title: Philip Morris International Inc. (PM) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Philip Morris International Inc. (PM). Economic Prospect Score: 62/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Philip Morris International Inc. is projected to grow FCF at 7% annually, reflecting its competitive position, sector dynamics, and historical compounding rate.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 8.5% WACC balances Philip Morris International Inc.'s earnings quality and balance sheet strength against sector volatility and macroeconomic risk factors.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal rate assumes long-run growth converges toward nominal GDP, appropriate for a mature S&P 500 constituent.

thesisTitle: Philip Morris International Inc.: A Fundamental Assessment
thesisParagraphs: Philip Morris International Inc. (PM) is a large-cap constituent of the S&P 500 with established market position and consistent cash flow generation. The company's competitive advantages and market share provide a degree of pricing power and earnings stability that supports a premium valuation.

My DCF applies a 7% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability and forward outlook, with a 8.5% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks and capital market conditions. The valuation is sensitive to assumptions about sustainable growth — investors should stress-test these inputs against their own convictions.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is Philip Morris International Inc.'s intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 7% FCF growth and 8.5% WACC, Opus calculates PM's intrinsic value at approximately $155.70 per share, suggesting the stock is undervalued at $135.00.

faq.question: What assumptions drive PM's valuation?
faq.answer: The key drivers are the FCF growth rate (7%) and discount rate (8.5%). A 1% increase in growth adds roughly 10-15% to the intrinsic value. These assumptions reflect Opus's independent assessment of the company's competitive position and risk profile.

faq.question: Should I buy or sell based on this analysis?
faq.answer: This is not financial advice. DCF models are inherently subjective — small changes in assumptions create large swings in output. Use this as one data point among many in your investment research process.

prospectVerdictDetail: Philip Morris is winning the tobacco harm reduction transition with IQOS heated tobacco and ZYN nicotine pouches. These products grow faster and carry better margins than combustible cigarettes. But regulatory risk is constant, and the ESG-unfriendly nature limits the investor base.
prospectKeyRisks: FDA or EU regulatory action restricting IQOS or flavored nicotine pouch sales
Illicit trade capturing market share in smoke-free product categories
Tax increases on heated tobacco and pouches narrowing the price advantage over combustible cigarettes
prospectKeyCatalysts: ZYN US demand exceeding manufacturing capacity, with new facility investments unlocking significant revenue growth
IQOS expanding into the US market, the world's most valuable tobacco market
Complete combustible cigarette phase-out in key markets positioning PM as a 'wellness' company and attracting ESG capital
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
